successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest with fields id, employeeId, leaveType, startDate, endDate, status, approverEmployeeId, and createdAt.

src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports a LeaveRepository interface whose methods operate on LeaveRequest from src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts and include create, findById, and findByEmployeeId.
src/modules/leave/postgres-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgresLeaveRepository implementing LeaveRepository using pg Pool.
A PostgreSQL migration exists for leave_requests with the specified columns, constraints, and indexes from the provided schema.
Vitest tests in tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.spec.ts verify persistence, retrieval by id, retrieval by employeeId, and correct LeaveRequest type usage.

constraints: All database access must go through repository interfaces (Repository pattern).
LeaveRepository and PostgresLeaveRepository must use LeaveRequest types imported from src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts.
PostgresLeaveRepository must implement the LeaveRepository contract.
Repository methods must return Promise-based results and handle async errors without unhandled promise rejections.
No sensitive data may be logged.
Any state-changing repository operation implemented in this phase must support the platform requirement that state-changing operations write an audit record.
Scope is limited to leave domain models, persistence contracts, PostgreSQL repository implementation, migration, and repository tests.
outOfScope: Fastify API endpoints for leave management
Service-layer business workflows beyond repository and model definitions
Any modules outside the leave domain
